Text:
Issue No. 24/2017 June EFY2017 Page 1
---
1. Summary
The Consumer Price Index (CPI), a key economic statistics, is surveyed, analyzed, and made available
to data users on a monthly basis.
The year-on-year, general inflation rate (annual percentage change) in the country stood at 13.9
percent for the month of June EFY2017. A year earlier (in June EFY2016), the rate was 19.9 percent.
The month-on-month general inflation rate increased by 0.5 percent for the month of June-EFY2017.
It was 0.9 percent in June EFY2016.
General inflation encompasses of two main divisions and twenty-one groups of expenditures. These
divisions registered the following price changes during the month of June EFY2017 compared with
June EFY2016.
2. Food & Non-alcoholic Beverages and Non-Food Inflation (Year-on-Year)
The year-on-year inflation and the month-to-month inflation rates helps to gauge the current infla-
tion trajectory, as they capture short-term fluctuations in prices and provides an immediate indicator
of inflationary pressures. Particularly, the month-to-month is also crucial for monitoring changes in
consumer prices and identifying any sudden spikes or declines in inflation.
The year-on-year inflation and the month-to-month inflation rates helps to gauge the current infla-
tion trajectory, as they capture short-term fluctuations in prices and provides an immediate indicator
of inflationary pressures. Particularly, the month-to-month is also crucial for monitoring changes in
consumer prices and identifying any sudden spikes or declines in inflation.
Food & Non-alcoholic Beverages index
The year-on-year inflation rate, also known as the annual inflation rate, measures the percentage
change in overall price levels from one year to the same month in the previous year. Accordingly, the
year-on-year inflation rate of the Food and Non-alcoholic component of the CPI, for the month of June
2017 EFY stood at 11.7%. This implies that the CPI of Food and Non-Alcoholic Beverages has, on aver-
age, increased by 11.7% compared to June EFY2016. The major contributing factors for the rise of food
inflation for June EFY2017 was the observed increase in the average prices of major food commodities
such as bread and cereals (0.0%), vegetables (14.9%), meat (12.0%), Sugar, jam, honey & chocolate
(16.5%), Milk, Cheese and Eggs (8.0%), Oils and Fats (29.2%), Fruit (14.3%), and Food products n.e.c
(18.8%).

0001: """
0002: dbt-specific pattern extraction helpers.
0003: 
0004: Uses regex (not tree-sitter) to extract dbt Jinja templating patterns from SQL files.
0005: These run on raw source text because dbt's Jinja is not valid SQL and is
0006: invisible to a standard SQL grammar.
0007: 
0008: Patterns extracted:
0009:   - {{ ref('model_name') }}           — intra-project model references
0010:   - {{ source('schema', 'table') }}   — raw source declarations
0011: 
0012: These are consumed by the Surveyor to build DBT_REF edges in the import graph,
0013: giving a meaningful graph for dbt projects where Python imports don't exist.
0014: """
0015: 
0016: from __future__ import annotations
0017: 
0018: import re
0019: 
0020: # Matches {{ ref('model') }} and {{ ref("model") }}
0021: _DBT_REF_RE = re.compile(r"\{\{\s*ref\(['\"]([^'\"]+)['\"]\)\s*\}\}")
0022: 
0023: # Matches {{ source('schema', 'table') }} and {{ source("schema", "table") }}
0024: _DBT_SOURCE_RE = re.compile(
0025:     r"\{\{\s*source\(['\"]([^'\"]+)['\"],\s*['\"]([^'\"]+)['\"]\)\s*\}\}"
0026: )
0027: 
0028: 
0029: def extract_dbt_refs(sql_text: str) -> list[str]:
0030:     """
0031:     Return all model names referenced via {{ ref('model_name') }} in *sql_text*.
0032: 
0033:     Example:
0034:         sql = "SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_orders') }} JOIN {{ ref('stg_customers') }}"
0035:         extract_dbt_refs(sql)  # → ['stg_orders', 'stg_customers']
0036:     """
0037:     return _DBT_REF_RE.findall(sql_text)
0038: 
0039: 
0040: def extract_dbt_sources(sql_text: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
0041:     """
0042:     Return (schema, table) pairs from {{ source('schema', 'table') }} calls.
0043: 
0044:     Example:
0045:         sql = "SELECT * FROM {{ source('raw', 'orders') }}"
0046:         extract_dbt_sources(sql)  # → [('raw', 'orders')]
0047:     """
0048:     return _DBT_SOURCE_RE.findall(sql_text)
--- END CODE ---
